title: ageratina altissima var. roanensis
intro: Ageratina altissima var. roanensis, also known as White Snakeroot, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its white flowers and distinctive white and purple stem coloration. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiseptic properties, which have been linked to various health benefits, including reduced fever, pain relief, and treatment of respiratory issues. White Snakeroot is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are said to promote relaxation, reduce anxiety, and support the immune system. However, it is essential to note that Ageratina altissima var. roanensis contains a toxic compound called tremetol, which can cause neurological symptoms if consumed in large quantities.
